heading: Producer eligibility
label: 1520 Producer eligibility
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

Amendments2008—, substituted “this subchapter” for “this chapter” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 110–246, § 12033(c)(2)(B)1994— substituted “Producer eligibility” for “Persons under twenty-one years of age” in section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no person shall be denied insurance under this chapter solely on the ground that he is under twenty-one years of age if such person is (1) over eighteen years of age, and (2) has a bona fide insurable interest in a crop as an owner-operator, landlord, tenant or sharecropper: , That any such person who enters into a Federal Crop Insurance contract shall be subject to the same legal liability and have the same legal rights with respect to such contract as any person over the age of twenty-one years.”Pub. L. 103–354Provided

Effective Date of 2008 AmendmentAmendment of this section and repeal of by effective , the date of enactment of , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4 of Pub. L. 110–246section 8701 of this titleMay 22, 2008

children.text: for purposes of catastrophic risk protection coverage, the producer is a “person” (as defined by the Secretary); and
for purposes of any other plan of insurance, the producer is 18 years of age and has a bona fide insurable interest in a crop as an owner-operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper.
